prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f18ec8cc`.
codex_response:
Implemented the `f18ec8cc` ARC2 task package. The rule is: split the input into contiguous vertical strips by per-column dominant color; if the strip colors are strictly increasing left-to-right, reverse the strip order, otherwise rotate the strips left by one, always preserving each strip’s internal noisy pixels. The verifier is in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f18ec8cc/verifier.py#L15), the strip helpers are in [helpers.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f18ec8cc/helpers.py#L6), and the generator is in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f18ec8cc/generator.py#L79).

I used the Opus hint and discarded the Sonnet hint; the correction is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f18ec8cc/notes.md#L1). No new primitive was needed, so `arc2/dsl_ext.py` was untouched. The generator matches the task family by sampling 3-4 vertical strips with official-like heights and widths, then injecting sparse cross-strip noise as one foreign pixel for each other strip color before applying the strip permutation.

Validation passed:
- `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task f18ec8cc` -> 6/6 official examples passed
- `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task f18ec8cc --n-examples 1000` -> built successfully
- `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task f18ec8cc` -> 1000/1000 generated examples passed
